issues.description: The loop bound changed from `0..n` to `0..=n`, so when `i == n` the body accesses `params[i]` (length n) and `vertex[i]` (length n), producing an out-of-bounds panic at runtime whenever `build_simplex` is called. This is a guaranteed runtime crash for every solver invocation, not just an edge case.
issues.suggested_fix: Revert the loop to `for i in 0..n`. A simplex in N-dimensional Nelder-Mead must have exactly N+1 vertices (1 initial + N perturbations), which is what the original loop produced.

issues.description: `build_simplex` gained a new required `scale: f64` parameter, but none of the existing call sites were updated. This is a compile error: `src/solver.rs:568` (the production caller inside the Nelder-Mead setup) and the unit tests at `src/solver.rs:2171`, `:2190`, `:2215` all still call `build_simplex(initial, &params)` with two arguments.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) give `scale` a default by making the public function call an inner helper with a constant 0.1 to preserve behavior, or (b) update every call site to pass `scale` explicitly (e.g., `build_simplex(initial, &problem.auto_params, 0.1)` and the three test calls).

issues.description: The existing test `simplex_has_n_plus_1_vertices` asserts that `build_simplex` returns exactly N+1 vertices for N=1,2,3. With the `0..=n` loop the function would (if it didn't panic first) return N+2 vertices, so this test must fail. The integration test `multi_param_warm_start_with_objective` (tests/solver_integration.rs:1078) also documents the 3+1 vertex contract. No test in the diff was updated or added to cover the new behavior.
issues.suggested_fix: Fix the loop bound. The existing test suite is correct about the Nelder-Mead contract — do not weaken it.

issues.description: The sole production caller of `build_simplex` passes the result directly into `NelderMead::new(simplex)`. argmin's Nelder-Mead implementation requires exactly `n+1` vertices for an `n`-parameter problem; feeding it a simplex of the wrong cardinality will either error out or produce undefined behavior. Combined with the off-by-one bug above, every solver call would panic or return `SolveResult::NoProgress`, regressing M3 auto-resolution entirely.
issues.suggested_fix: Once the off-by-one is fixed, re-run `cargo test -p reify-constraints` to confirm the Nelder-Mead solver still converges on the integration suite.

issues.description: `validate_simplex` is introduced but never called anywhere in the workspace (verified via grep — zero references outside the definition itself). In a workspace that treats `#![deny(dead_code)]` / `-D warnings` as CI-blocking, this will fail the build. Even setting lint policy aside, adding an unused helper is the over-engineering pattern called out in `feedback_no_throwaway.md`.
issues.suggested_fix: Either delete `validate_simplex`, or actually wire it in at the call site (e.g., `debug_assert!(validate_simplex(&simplex, problem.auto_params.len()))` in `run_solver` after building the simplex). If you wire it in, note that its current predicate (`len() == n + 1`) would contradict the new `0..=n` loop — another reason the loop change is wrong.

issues.description: The doc comment still says "Creates N+1 vertices: the initial point plus N perturbations," but the new loop (if it didn't panic) would create N+2 vertices. After fixing the loop bound this becomes a non-issue; if the intent really were to create N+2, the doc would need updating and so would every caller's expectations.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the N+1 doc and revert the loop; alternatively, if a different cardinality is genuinely desired, update the doc, the capacity hint (`Vec::with_capacity(n + 1)`), the existing `simplex_has_n_plus_1_vertices` test, and the Nelder-Mead caller.

issues.description: The new `scale` parameter is unvalidated. A caller passing `0.0` produces a degenerate simplex (all perturbed vertices coincide with `initial` after clamping), which silently breaks Nelder-Mead convergence without an error. Negative or NaN values are similarly unguarded.
issues.suggested_fix: Either constrain the type (e.g., accept only positive finite scales and `debug_assert!(scale.is_finite() && scale > 0.0)`) or document the precondition and keep a single internal constant (0.1) rather than exposing the knob until a caller actually needs it. If no caller needs it yet, don't add the parameter — YAGNI.

issues.description: No tests cover the new `scale` parameter or `validate_simplex`. The only existing tests check vertex count, not perturbation magnitude, so the behavioral change of swapping `0.1` for a parameter is completely unverified.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a unit test that asserts each perturbed vertex differs from `initial` by `(hi - lo) * scale` in exactly one dimension, and a test for `validate_simplex` covering (a) correct N+1 shape, (b) wrong outer length, (c) wrong inner length.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: This diff is not shippable. The change to `for i in 0..=n` causes a guaranteed out-of-bounds panic on `params[i]`/`vertex[i]` when `i == n`, and the new required `scale` parameter breaks four existing call sites (one production, three tests) without updating any of them — the crate will not even compile. Even if those were fixed, the new loop would produce N+2 vertices, violating the Nelder-Mead contract and the existing `simplex_has_n_plus_1_vertices` test. On top of the correctness bugs, `validate_simplex` is dead code (and its own predicate contradicts the new build logic), the doc comment is now stale, the `scale` parameter is unvalidated, and there are no tests for either addition. Revert the loop change, either drop `scale` or thread it through every caller with validation, and either remove or actually wire in `validate_simplex`.
